21n9eD20mU.insight:
DO NOT get baited by <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Revitalize" />! It may sound OP with <ScChampionInline championId="Aatrox" />'s drain-tank playstyle, but it doesn't heal enough for it to be worth it.

* Run <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Overgrowth" /> as your default, pivot to <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Unflinching" /> vs champions who CC during trades like <ScChampionInline championId="Riven" />, <ScChampionInline championId="Ornn" />, and <ScChampionInline championId="Chogath" />.
* <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Bone Plating" /> vs burst traders, <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Second Wind" /> otherwise. 
* The way that <ScChampionInline championId="Aatrox" />'s <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> works is that it grants him healing, scaling off of his bonus HP. So <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Overgrowth" /> does actually directly provide him with more healing - usually about 2-3% once you've reached 15+ stacks and have the bonus 3.5% HP effect.

g2ZMwTe0ZT.insight: is the ideal keystone for . He scales amazingly well with the AD, and when fully stacked it lets him pop off in teamfights, chaining resets and healing through everything. * Despite running , usually loses extended fights vs champs like , , and . Kite away once your spells are on cooldown instead of auto-attacking. * You can commit to longer trades if your opponent runs a weak rune like or , since your autos still deal solid damage with stacked.

uiWVv5l1ZH.insight:
Despite most of <ScChampionInline championId="Aatrox" />'s power being in his <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" />'s, he still benefits a lot from weaving autos in fights to proc his passive or <ScItemInline itemName="Sundered Sky" /> for huge burst healing.

* <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Legend: Alacrity" /> makes his auto-weaving between <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" />'s smoother, letting you get those big burst heals quickly, without feeling like you're stuck forever in an auto animation.
* Even though <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Legend: Haste" /> would be great, you can't build Attack Speed from your items, so it's good to get it from your runes.

runesv3.summonerSpellExplanation: Can swap to <ScSummonerInline summonerIdentifier="Teleport" /> in tough or un-interactive lanes like <ScChampionInline championId="Quinn" />, <ScChampionInline championId="Malphite" />.

itemGroups.insight: Always rush first. Swap for against auto-heavy champs or for against magic-damage-heavy comps. Swap for against heavy bruiser or tank comps.
itemGroups.title: Core Build

itemGroups.insight: Build <ScItemInline itemName="Serylda's Grudge" /> vs squishies for better finishing power. <ScItemInline itemName="Endless Hunger" /> for sustained fighting. <ScItemInline itemName="Sterak's Gage" /> to counter burst. Always go <ScItemInline itemName="Sterak's Gage" /> either 4th or 5th
itemGroups.title: Fourth Item Options

itemGroups.insight: <ScItemInline itemName="Sterak's Gage" /> if you haven't built it yet. <ScItemInline itemName="Death's Dance" /> for Armor. <ScItemInline itemName="Spirit Visage" /> or <ScItemInline itemName="Force of Nature" /> for MR.
itemGroups.title: Fifth Item Options

itemGroups.insight: <ScItemInline itemName="Guardian Angel" /> should be your default 6th item most games, unless you really need the other 2. Helps secure the final fight of the game.
itemGroups.title: Sixth Item Options

startingItems.sectionExplainer: Start <ScItemInline itemName="Doran's Shield" />  into ranged match-ups for sustain

insights.title: Extending Your Range
insights.reason:
The downside of <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" /> is that it normally has a lot less range than <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q1" />. But you can get around this by angling your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" /> to the side instead of aiming it straight at them.

* <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" possessive />'s sweet-spot is curved, and the corners of that curve reach further out than the center does. So if you aim <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" /> about 30 degrees to the side of your target, the far edge ends up reaching almost as far as <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q1" />.
* Basically, stop aiming <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" /> directly at people. Aim it off to the side so they get clipped by the edge. You get almost <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q1" /> range but with <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" possessive />'s higher damage.

insights.title: Dump Your Q1
insights.reason:
One of your biggest vulnerabilities as <ScChampionInline championId="Aatrox" /> is being jumped on immediately in fights. Because your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q1" /> sweet-spot is so far away, it's hard to peel yourself.

* DON'T PANIC. Immediately use <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q1" />, just to get it out of the way. It's useless in close range. Now you can play the fight out.
* It's much easier to land <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" /> combined with your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> in close range. You can <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q2" /> and <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> backwards, securing the sweetspot. If you don't think you can land sweetspot, then save it for <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q3" /> - <ScComboInline steps={["Q3", "E"]} /> is much easier to land than any of the other Q's and deals the highest damage.
